frameworks.name: default mode network
frameworks.one_line: Network whose activation typically decreases during externally focused tasks

frameworks.name: internally-directed / self-referential cognition account
frameworks.one_line: Views DMN activity as reflecting inward, self-relevant thought

frameworks.name: context / scene representation account
frameworks.one_line: Proposes DMN integrates spatial, self-referential, and temporal information into context

core_question: What role does the default mode network play during cognitive transitions such as task switches and rests?
key_assumptions: DMN activity is meaningfully reflected by fMRI BOLD changes
Task switches, rests, and restarts can be operationalized within one paradigm
DMN activity is dissociable from purely externally focused task engagement
manipulations: Task switch versus task maintenance trials
Brief rest periods inserted between task blocks
key_findings: Large DMN increases for brief rests
Large DMN increases for task restarts after rest
interpretations: DMN encodes scene, episode, or context by integrating spatial, self-referential, and temporal information
Context representations are strong at rest and re-engaged at major cognitive transitions
